children.heading: Identification of screening-eligible WTC survivors and certified-eligible WTC survivors
children.label: a Identification of screening-eligible WTC survivors and certified-eligible WTC survivors

children.heading: Currently identified survivor
children.label: i Currently identified survivor
children.text: An individual, including a WTC responder, who has been identified as eligible for medical treatment and monitoring by the WTC Environmental Health Center as of .January 2, 2011

children.heading: Survivor who meets current eligibility criteria
children.label: ii Survivor who meets current eligibility criteria
children.text: An individual who is not a WTC responder, for purposes of the initial health evaluation under subsection (b), claims symptoms of a WTC-related health condition and meets any of the current eligibility criteria described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Survivor who meets modified eligibility criteria
children.label: iii Survivor who meets modified eligibility criteria
children.text: An individual who is not a WTC responder, for purposes of the initial health evaluation under subsection (b), claims symptoms of a WTC-related health condition and meets such eligibility criteria relating to exposure to airborne toxins, other hazards, or adverse conditions resulting from the , terrorist attacks as the WTC Administrator determines, after consultation with the Data Centers described in and the WTC Scientific/Technical Advisory Committee and WTC Health Program Steering Committees under .September 11, 2001section 300mm–4 of this titlesection 300mm–1 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: A person who was present in the New York City disaster area in the dust or dust cloud on .September 11, 2001

children.label: I
children.text: at least 4 days during the 4-month period beginning on , and ending on ; orSeptember 11, 2001January 10, 2002

children.label: II
children.text: at least 30 days during the period beginning on , and ending on .September 11, 2001July 31, 2002

children.label: iii
children.text: Any person who worked as a cleanup worker or performed maintenance work in the New York City disaster area during the 4-month period described in subparagraph (B)(i) and had extensive exposure to WTC dust as a result of such work.

children.label: iv
children.text: A person who was deemed eligible to receive a grant from the Lower Manhattan Development Corporation Residential Grant Program, who possessed a lease for a residence or purchased a residence in the New York City disaster area, and who resided in such residence during the period beginning on , and ending on .September 11, 2001May 31, 2003

children.label: I
children.text: at any time during the period beginning on , and ending on , was in the New York City disaster area; andSeptember 11, 2001May 31, 2003

children.label: II
children.text: was deemed eligible to receive a grant from the Lower Manhattan Development Corporation WTC Small Firms Attraction and Retention Act program or other government incentive program designed to revitalize the lower Manhattan economy after the , terrorist attacks.September 11, 2001

children.heading: Application and determination process for screening eligibility
children.label: C Application and determination process for screening eligibility
I
children.text: there shall be no fee charged to the applicant for making an application for such determination;

children.label: II
children.text: the Administrator shall make a determination on such an application not later than 60 days after the date of filing the application;

children.label: aa
children.text: based on the application submitted, the individual does not meet the eligibility criteria; or

children.label: bb
children.text: the numerical limitation on certifications of certified-eligible WTC survivors set forth in paragraph (3) has been met; and

children.label: IV
children.text: an individual who is determined not to be a screening-eligible WTC survivor shall have an opportunity to appeal such determination in a manner established under such process.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: In the case of an individual who is described in subparagraph (A)(i) or who is determined under clause (i) (consistent with paragraph (3)) to be a screening-eligible WTC survivor, the WTC Program Administrator shall provide an appropriate written documentation of such fact.

children.heading: Currently identified survivors
children.label: aa Currently identified survivors
children.text: In the case of an individual who is described in subparagraph (A)(i), the WTC Program Administrator shall provide the written documentation under subclause (I) not later than .July 1, 2011

children.heading: Other members
children.label: bb Other members
children.text: In the case of another individual who is determined under clause (i) and consistent with paragraph (3) to be a screening-eligible WTC survivor, the WTC Program Administrator shall provide the written documentation under subclause (I) at the time of such determination.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: A Definition
children.text: The term “certified-eligible WTC survivor” means, subject to paragraph (3), a screening-eligible WTC survivor who the WTC Program Administrator certifies under subparagraph (B) to be eligible for followup monitoring and treatment under this subpart.

children.heading: Certification of eligibility for monitoring and treatment
children.label: B Certification of eligibility for monitoring and treatment

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The WTC Program Administrator shall establish a certification process under which the Administrator shall provide appropriate certification to screening-eligible WTC survivors who, pursuant to the initial health evaluation under subsection (b), are determined to be eligible for followup monitoring and treatment under this subpart.

children.heading: Currently identified survivors
children.label: I Currently identified survivors
children.text: In the case of an individual who is described in paragraph (1)(A)(i), the WTC Program Administrator shall provide the certification under clause (i) not later than .July 1, 2011

children.heading: Other members
children.label: II Other members
children.text: In the case of another individual who is determined under clause (i) to be eligible for followup monitoring and treatment, the WTC Program Administrator shall provide the certification under such clause at the time of such determination.

children.heading: Numerical limitation on certified-eligible WTC survivors
children.label: 3 Numerical limitation on certified-eligible WTC survivors

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The total number of individuals not described in paragraph (1)(A)(i) who may be certified as certified-eligible WTC survivors under paragraph (2)(B) shall not exceed 75,000 at any time.

children.label: I
children.text: in accordance with such subparagraph; and

children.label: II
children.text: to such number, as determined by the Administrator based on the best available information and subject to amounts made available under this subchapter, that will ensure sufficient funds will be available to provide treatment and monitoring benefits under this subchapter, with respect to all individuals receiving such certifications; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provide priority in such certifications in the order in which individuals apply for a determination under paragraph (2)(B).

children.heading: Disqualification of individuals on terrorist watch list
children.label: 4 Disqualification of individuals on terrorist watch list
children.text: No individual who is on the terrorist watch list maintained by the Department of Homeland Security shall qualify as a screening-eligible WTC survivor or a certified-eligible WTC survivor. Before determining any individual to be a screening-eligible WTC survivor under paragraph (1) or certifying any individual as a certified eligible WTC survivor under paragraph (2), the Administrator, in consultation with the Secretary of Homeland Security, shall determine whether the individual is on such list.1So in original. Probably should be “certified-eligible”.1

children.heading: Initial health evaluation to determine eligibility for followup monitoring or treatment
children.label: b Initial health evaluation to determine eligibility for followup monitoring or treatment

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of a screening-eligible WTC survivor, the WTC Program shall provide for an initial health evaluation to determine if the survivor has a WTC-related health condition and is eligible for followup monitoring and treatment benefits under the WTC Program. Initial health evaluation protocols under shall be subject to approval by the WTC Program Administrator.section 300mm–4(a)(2)(A)(ii) of this title

children.heading: Initial health evaluation providers
children.label: 2 Initial health evaluation providers
children.text: The initial health evaluation described in paragraph (1) shall be provided through a Clinical Center of Excellence with respect to the individual involved.

children.heading: Limitation on initial health evaluation benefits
children.label: 3 Limitation on initial health evaluation benefits
children.text: Benefits for an initial health evaluation under this subpart for a screening-eligible WTC survivor shall consist only of a single medical initial health evaluation consistent with initial health evaluation protocols described in paragraph (1). Nothing in this paragraph shall be construed as preventing such an individual from seeking additional medical initial health evaluations at the expense of the individual.
